symbols.description: Traditional Zohar-style typographic device where the word Hadrash/Derasha is split and rearranged with parentheses on both sides

symbols.position: flanking the two halves of the title

mixed_media_regions.description: Large ornamental Hebrew typography combining the word 'Hadrash' split into two halves with parentheses and a small letter 'ע' (ayin) between them, characteristic of Zohar page openings

uncertain: The Rashi-script body text in both paragraphs cannot be read reliably — multiple letters are ambiguous due to similar Rashi glyphs.
First paragraph (approx. 6 lines) – text appears to discuss 'מעשה חסידים' and 'בזמן ההוא' but specific words are not confident.
Second paragraph (approx. 12 lines) – appears to be a continuous Zohar commentary with frequent words 'דרושא', 'מארי', 'אורייתא', but individual word boundaries are not certain.
The small letter between the two title halves: could be 'ע' (ayin) or possibly a decorative mark — uncertain.
Possible line-break/hyphenation marks in the second paragraph are unclear.
